#!/bin/bash

setup_configs() {
	local config_dir="$HOME/.config"
	local repo_dir="$HOME/.dotfiles/dotfiles"

	# List of configuration directories and files
	local configs=(
		"fish"
		"kitty"
		"nvim"
    "awesome"
    "picom"
    "rofi"
    "lazygit"
    "btop"
    "zellij"
    "zed"
    "yazi"
    "gtk-3.0"
	)

	# Remove existing configurations
	for config in "${configs[@]}"; do
		rm -rf "${config_dir:?}/${config:?}"
	done

	# Create symbolic links
	for config in "${configs[@]}"; do
		ln -sf "$repo_dir/$config" "$config_dir/$config"
	done

	echo "Configurations have been set up."
}

setup_home_configs() {
	local home_configs=(
		"xinitrc:.xinitrc"
	)

	for entry in "${home_configs[@]}"; do
		src="${entry%%:*}"
		dest="${entry##*:}"
		rm -f "$HOME/$dest"
		ln -sf "$HOME/.dotfiles/dotfiles/$src" "$HOME/$dest"
	done

	echo "Home configurations have been set up."
}

setup_dmenu_scripts() {
	local scripts_dir="$HOME/.dotfiles/dotfiles/dmenu_scripts"
	local bin_dir="$HOME/.local/bin"

	mkdir -p "$bin_dir"

	for script in "$scripts_dir"/*; do
		name=$(basename "$script")
		ln -sf "$script" "$bin_dir/$name"
		chmod +x "$script"
	done

	echo "Dmenu scripts have been set up."
}

# Call the functions
setup_configs
setup_home_configs
setup_dmenu_scripts
exec fish

# ============================================================================
# GENERAL SETTINGS
# ============================================================================

# Change prefix from Ctrl+b to Ctrl+a (more ergonomic, similar to screen)
unbind C-b
set -g prefix C-a
bind C-a send-prefix

# Remove delay when pressing escape key (important for vim/neovim)
set -s escape-time 0

# Set scrollback buffer to 20000 lines (default is 2000)
set -g history-limit 20000

# Enable mouse support for scrolling, selecting panes, and resizing
set -g mouse on

# Send focus events to applications running in tmux
set -g focus-events on

# Resize windows to smallest client currently viewing (not smallest attached session)
setw -g aggressive-resize on

# Don't detach tmux when destroying a session (switch to another session)
set -g detach-on-destroy off

# Increase display time for messages (default is 750ms)
set -g display-time 2000

# Increase display time for pane indicators (default is 1000ms)
set -g display-panes-time 3000


# ============================================================================
# TERMINAL & COLOR SETTINGS
# ============================================================================

# Set default terminal type to support modern terminal features
set -g default-terminal "${TERM}"

# Enable true color (RGB) support for all terminals
set -as terminal-features ",*:RGB"

# Enable true color support specifically for xterm-256color terminals
set-option -ga terminal-overrides ",xterm-256color:Tc"


# ============================================================================
# WINDOW & PANE SETTINGS
# ============================================================================

# Start window numbering at 1 instead of 0 (easier to reach on keyboard)
set -g base-index 1

# Start pane numbering at 1 instead of 0
setw -g pane-base-index 1

# Automatically renumber windows when one is closed (keeps numbering sequential)
set -g renumber-windows on

# New windows and panes open in current directory
bind c new-window -c "#{pane_current_path}"
bind '"' split-window -c "#{pane_current_path}"
bind % split-window -h -c "#{pane_current_path}"


# ============================================================================
# WINDOW TITLE & NAMING
# ============================================================================

# Allow tmux to set the terminal window title
set -g set-titles on

# Allow programs to rename windows automatically based on running command
set -g allow-rename on


# ============================================================================
# ACTIVITY & NOTIFICATIONS
# ============================================================================

# Highlight windows with activity in the status bar
setw -g monitor-activity on

# Ring bell in any window (not just current)
set -g bell-action none

# Disable visual bell (no screen flash on bell)
set -g visual-bell on

# Disable visual notification for window activity
set -g visual-activity off


# ============================================================================
# KEY BINDINGS
# ============================================================================

# Use vim-style keybindings in copy mode (hjkl navigation, etc.)
setw -g mode-keys vi

# Reload configuration file
bind r source-file ~/.config/tmux/tmux.conf \; display "Config reloaded!"

# Split window keybindings (| for vertical, - for horizontal)
bind | split-window -h -c "#{pane_current_path}"
bind - split-window -v -c "#{pane_current_path}"

# Vim-style pane navigation
bind h select-pane -L
bind j select-pane -D
bind k select-pane -U
bind l select-pane -R

# Vim-style pane resizing
bind -r H resize-pane -L 5
bind -r J resize-pane -D 5
bind -r K resize-pane -U 5
bind -r L resize-pane -R 5

# Quick window navigation
bind -r C-h select-window -t :-
bind -r C-l select-window -t :+

# Toggle pane synchronization (type in all panes at once)
bind S set-window-option synchronize-panes\; display "Sync: #{?synchronize-panes,ON,OFF}"

# Break pane into new window
bind b break-pane -d

# Create new session
bind C-c new-session

# Find session
bind C-f command-prompt -p find-session 'switch-client -t %%'

# Kill pane without confirmation
bind x kill-pane

# Kill window without confirmation
bind X kill-window


# ============================================================================
# COPY MODE & CLIPBOARD
# ============================================================================

# Enter copy mode with PREFIX + [ or PREFIX + Escape
bind Escape copy-mode

# Use 'v' to begin selection (like in vim)
bind-key -T copy-mode-vi v send-keys -X begin-selection

# Use 'V' for line selection
bind-key -T copy-mode-vi V send-keys -X select-line

# Use 'C-v' for rectangle selection
bind-key -T copy-mode-vi C-v send-keys -X rectangle-toggle

# Use 'y' to copy selection to system clipboard
# For macOS
if-shell "uname | grep -q Darwin" {
  bind-key -T copy-mode-vi y send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel "pbcopy"
}
# For Linux with xclip
if-shell "uname | grep -q Linux && command -v xclip > /dev/null" {
  bind-key -T copy-mode-vi y send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel "xclip -selection clipboard -i"
}
# For Linux with xsel
if-shell "uname | grep -q Linux && command -v xsel > /dev/null" {
  bind-key -T copy-mode-vi y send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel "xsel -ib"
}

# Copy mouse selections directly to system clipboard (macOS)
bind -T copy-mode-vi MouseDragEnd1Pane send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel "pbcopy"

# Use PREFIX + p to paste from tmux's internal buffer
bind p paste-buffer

# Use PREFIX + P to choose which buffer to paste
bind P choose-buffer

# Vim-style search in copy mode
bind-key -T copy-mode-vi / command-prompt -p "search down" "send -X search-forward \"%%%\""
bind-key -T copy-mode-vi ? command-prompt -p "search up" "send -X search-backward \"%%%\""

# Vim-style incremental search
bind-key -T copy-mode-vi n send-keys -X search-again
bind-key -T copy-mode-vi N send-keys -X search-reverse


# ============================================================================
# STATUS BAR
# ============================================================================

# Update status bar every 5 seconds
set -g status-interval 5

# Center the window list
set -g status-justify centre

# Status bar colors
set -g status-style bg=black,fg=white

# Active window style
setw -g window-status-current-style bg=blue,fg=white,bold

# Pane border colors
set -g pane-border-style fg=colour240
set -g pane-active-border-style fg=blue

# Message style
set -g message-style bg=colour235,fg=colour166

# Left status: session name
set -g status-left-length 40
set -g status-left "#[fg=green]Session: #S #[fg=yellow]#I #[fg=cyan]#P"

# Right status: prefix highlight + date and time
set -g status-right-length 60
set -g status-right "#{prefix_highlight} #[fg=cyan]%d %b %R"


# ============================================================================
# PLUGINS
# ============================================================================

# Tmux Plugin Manager (TPM)
set -g @plugin 'tmux-plugins/tpm'

# Sensible tmux defaults
set -g @plugin 'tmux-plugins/tmux-sensible'

# Better clipboard integration
set -g @plugin 'tmux-plugins/tmux-yank'

# Save and restore tmux sessions across reboots
set -g @plugin 'tmux-plugins/tmux-resurrect'

# Auto-save tmux sessions every 15 minutes
set -g @plugin 'tmux-plugins/tmux-continuum'

# Show when prefix key is pressed
set -g @plugin 'tmux-plugins/tmux-prefix-highlight'

# Smart session manager with fuzzy finder
set -g @plugin 'joshmedeski/tmux-nerd-font-window-name'
set -g @plugin 'joshmedeski/sesh'

# Plugin settings
# ----------------------------------------

# Tmux-continuum: automatic restore
set -g @continuum-restore 'on'

# Tmux-continuum: save interval (in minutes)
set -g @continuum-save-interval '15'

# Tmux-resurrect: restore vim sessions
set -g @resurrect-strategy-vim 'session'

# Tmux-resurrect: restore neovim sessions
set -g @resurrect-strategy-nvim 'session'

# Tmux-resurrect: restore pane contents
set -g @resurrect-capture-pane-contents 'on'

# Tmux-resurrect: run custom commands after restore
set -g @resurrect-hook-post-restore-all '~/.config/tmux/resurrect-post-restore.sh'

#!/bin/bash

setup_configs() {
	local config_dir="$HOME/.config"
	local repo_dir="$HOME/.dotfiles/dotfiles"

	# List of configuration directories and files
	local configs=(
		"fish"
		"kitty"
		"nvim"
    "awesome"
    "picom"
    "rofi"
    "lazygit"
    "btop"
    "zellij"
    "zed"
    "yazi"
    "gtk-3.0"
	)

	# Remove existing configurations
	for config in "${configs[@]}"; do
		rm -rf "${config_dir:?}/${config:?}"
	done

	# Create symbolic links
	for config in "${configs[@]}"; do
		ln -sf "$repo_dir/$config" "$config_dir/$config"
	done

	echo "Configurations have been set up."
}

setup_home_configs() {
	local home_configs=(
		"xinitrc:.xinitrc"
	)

	for entry in "${home_configs[@]}"; do
		src="${entry%%:*}"
		dest="${entry##*:}"
		rm -f "$HOME/$dest"
		ln -sf "$HOME/.dotfiles/dotfiles/$src" "$HOME/$dest"
	done

	echo "Home configurations have been set up."
}

setup_dmenu_scripts() {
	local scripts_dir="$HOME/.dotfiles/dotfiles/dmenu_scripts"
	local bin_dir="$HOME/.local/bin"

	mkdir -p "$bin_dir"

	for script in "$scripts_dir"/*; do
		name=$(basename "$script")
		ln -sf "$script" "$bin_dir/$name"
		chmod +x "$script"
	done

	echo "Dmenu scripts have been set up."
}

# Call the functions
setup_configs
setup_home_configs
setup_dmenu_scripts
exec fish
